label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out if following these instructions brings me back to the starting point. I need to break it down step by step. Let me start by imagining I'm standing at the starting point facing a certain direction, maybe north. First instruction: Take 7 steps. So I move forward 7 steps in my initial direction (north). Now I'm 7 steps north of the start. Second instruction: Take 8 steps. Continuing in the same direction (north), I take 8 more steps. Now I'm 7 + 8 = 15 steps north of the starting point. Third instruction: Take 10 steps. Still facing north, I take 10 more steps. Total distance from start is now 15 + 10 = 25 steps north. Fourth instruction: Turn around. Turning around means I change direction. If I was facing north, now I'm facing south. Fifth instruction: Turn around again. Turning around once more would make me face north again. So after two turns, I'm back to facing north. Sixth instruction: Take 5 steps. Now moving north again, I take 5 steps. Total from start is 25 (previous north steps) + 5 = 30 steps north. Seventh instruction: Turn around. Now facing south again. At the end of these instructions, I'm facing south and located 30 steps north of the starting point. I haven't taken any steps after the last turn around, so I'm not moving from that position. Therefore, I'm 30 steps away from the starting point, facing south. I have not returned to the starting point. Wait, let me double-check my reasoning to make sure I didn't make any errors. Starting at the origin, facing north: - Take 7 steps north → position = +7 - Take 8 steps north → position = +15 - Take 10 steps north → position = +25 - Turn around → facing
